summary: Iteration 26 — Verification of AuthContext self-heal + AccountPage UID display fix (frontend-only, no account creation). All render-sanity checks PASS. /login and /signup expose the full set of data-testids; /account NEVER prints a 28-character Firebase UID — it prints exactly 'ID: Activating…' when no customer_id is yet allocated (guest visit). Public pages (/, /expo, /trade-news, /tools) load without runtime errors and the AuthContext change did not white-screen the app. Full login/signup was not attempted per instructions (external DO auth backend is CORS-blocked from the Emergent preview host; documented environment limitation).

verified_scenarios.evidence: data-testids login-form, login-identifier, login-password, login-submit, login-google, login-forgot-link, login-signup-link all present. No pageerror. (One 401 in console = DO auth backend CORS-blocked in preview — expected.)
data-testids signup-form, signup-name, signup-email, signup-password, signup-submit, signup-google, signup-role all present. No pageerror.

verified_scenarios.scenario: GET /account (guest) does not crash and never shows Firebase UID
verified_scenarios.evidence: account-header + account-uid render. account-uid text = 'ID: Activating…' — contains 'Activating', contains NO 28-char alphanumeric Firebase UID, contains no 5-digit customer ID (as expected for guest). Page renders full Guest Trader shell with 'Sign in to sync' CTA rather than redirecting — this is by design (AccountPage.jsx uses /api/account/me guest session and only auth-gates the sync CTA).

AuthContext.jsx lines 33–49: the self-heal only fires when `!profile.customer_id && profile._source === 'legacy_hydrate'`, is guarded by healRef so it runs once per uid/session, calls POST /onboarding/register then re-fetches /v1/profiles/{uid}, swallows errors so a failure won't crash the app. The last-write-wins guard (seqRef + setAccount((prev) => ...)) preserves a known-good customer_id if a subsequent placeholder response arrives. This path cannot be executed in preview because Firebase login → DO backend is CORS-blocked; verifiable only on leadnation.app.

verified_scenarios.scenario: AccountPage never renders raw Firebase UID
verified_scenarios.evidence: AccountPage.jsx line 83+105: custId = account?.user?.customer_id || p.customerId || p.customer_id || '' ; render is `{custId ? custId : 'Activating…'}`. Firebase UID (fbUser.uid / auth.currentUser.uid) is never bound to the display. Confirmed at runtime: guest /account shows literal 'Activating…'.

verified_scenarios.scenario: No regressions on public pages after AuthContext change
verified_scenarios.evidence: / (body 4435 chars, 0 console errors), /expo (body 2821 chars, 1 expected 401 from DO profiles), /trade-news (body 2958 chars, 0 errors), /tools (body 2469 chars, 0 errors, 'Eight tools. One unfair advantage.' hero renders). AuthProvider wraps the whole app — none of these pages white-screen, confirming the refreshAccount change did not break the provider.

critical_code_review_comments: AuthContext.jsx L37 – needsHeal condition is strict (profile._source === 'legacy_hydrate'). If the DO backend ever changes the placeholder source label (e.g. 'placeholder', 'stub', or drops the _source key altogether), the self-heal will silently no-op. Consider heal-if-authenticated-and-no-customer-id after N seconds, gated on `!profile?.customer_id` alone with a 2-3s debounce, or add a version guard tied to the DO backend's documented shape.

AuthContext.jsx L48 – the self-heal catch is `catch (_) {}`, swallowing 4xx/5xx from /onboarding/register. If register keeps failing (e.g. Firebase token rejected, DO backend rate-limits), the user will never see any hint — the UI will stay on 'Activating…' forever. Suggest surfacing a subtle 'Account activation retrying…' state or logging via analytics so E1 can detect stuck accounts.

AuthContext.jsx L38 – healRef.current stores only the uid, so the once-per-session guard resets on tab reload but never retries within the same session after a transient network failure. If /onboarding/register 502s once, the user's session will never self-heal until they refresh. Consider clearing healRef on error so a subsequent refreshAccount can retry.

AuthContext.jsx L37 – uses `!profile?.customer_id` which treats customer_id === 0 as needs-heal. Not a real risk (customer IDs are 5-digit strings starting at 00001), but if the DO backend ever returns numeric 0 as a placeholder it would infinitely try to heal. Prefer `profile?.customer_id == null || profile?.customer_id === ''`.

AccountPage.jsx L105 – displays 'Activating…' whenever custId is falsy, including for a plain guest who has never signed in (they will see 'Activating…' rather than something like 'Sign in to get an ID'). Not a bug per the acceptance criteria (which only forbids Firebase UID leakage), but slightly misleading UX for anonymous visitors. Consider `custId || (isAuthed ? 'Activating…' : 'Guest')`.

success_rate.frontend: 100% for the render-sanity + no-UID-leak checks in scope (6/6 verified scenarios PASS). Auth-flow self-heal is code-verified but runtime-untestable in preview.

test_credentials: Not used — instructions explicitly forbade creating accounts, and admin login is CORS-blocked in preview.
seed_data_creation: None. No accounts created, no records inserted.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The AuthContext self-heal branch is guarded by `_source === 'legacy_hydrate'` — that string is defined by the external DO backend (leadnation-lfrhs.ondigitalocean.app) and cannot be exercised in the Emergent preview because CORS blocks Firebase login to that backend. Any future testing of the self-heal MUST happen on production (leadnation.app) with a freshly-created half-registered account (Firebase user exists, but no row in DO `profiles`). Verify by (a) signing in, (b) confirming AccountPage shows 'Activating…' briefly, (c) confirming it flips to a 5-digit Customer ID within ~1s, (d) confirming Network tab shows a POST /onboarding/register followed by a GET /v1/profiles/{uid} returning customer_id. Also confirm that a second refresh does NOT re-POST /onboarding/register (healRef guard).
rca of the issue: Not applicable — this iteration is a verification pass for a targeted fix. The reported root cause (DO backend returning `{uid:'0', customer_id:null, _source:'legacy_hydrate'}` for half-registered Firebase users) is addressed by AuthContext.refreshAccount self-healing via POST /onboarding/register once per uid, and by AccountPage.jsx binding the ID label to `account.user.customer_id` (falling back to the literal text 'Activating…') instead of the raw Firebase UID. Both code paths are present and correct; runtime confirmation of the auth-dependent branch requires production.
